original_image_tags: photograph of the Duchess of Kent presenting the men's doubles cup to a man in a blazer and white trousers, standing on a grass court. A crowd is visible in the background.

photograph of Maureen Connolly and Louise Brough at the net. Caption below reads: "WINNING SMILE from Maureen Connolly and 'Well done!' from Louise Brough. America's Little Mo had just beaten Louise 7–5, 6–3 to win the women's singles."

photograph of Australia's Ken McGregor (left) covering Frank Sedgman. Caption reads: "Australia's Ken McGregor (left) covers Frank Sedgman. The world's greatest doubles pair easily won another title."

photograph of two women on a tennis court. Caption reads: "WINNERS' ACTION.—Little Mo (left) all out for a winner. LOSER'S REACTION.—Louise Brough all in after the match. Mo and the heat were too much."
diagram of the Brooks Appliance, showing a truss-like device.
comic strip panel titled "MUM", showing a woman at a shop counter with a shopkeeper.

advertisement for Toni Home Perm. Caption reads: "Because they've proved a Toni perm looks perfect from the first day." "Because they've proved a Toni perm stays perfect for months and months." "More women choose Toni than all other home perms combined!" "Whale Head Refill 9/-" "Toni Home Perm"
sketch of a craft on the ground. Caption reads: "The saucer begins to take off: the outer disk whirs faster and faster, rising up the centre 'conning-tower,' which remains stationary."
sketch of a craft rising into the air. Caption reads: "Airborne, the strange craft whirls off at terrific speed, soon to be out of sight. The flame around the central tower rose again."
photograph of a man in uniform standing near a large concrete structure. Caption reads: "Hole in ground"
photograph of a man with a beard and a hat. Caption reads: "Mr. George Edwards, Chief Designer for Vickers-Armstrongs"
comic strip panel titled "CAESAR", showing a dog with a thought bubble saying "(Ball)dogged does it!"
